frameworks.name: recurrent gain control
frameworks.one_line: intracortical recurrent normalization shapes V1 dynamics into a suppressive traveling wave

frameworks.name: apparent motion correspondence problem
frameworks.one_line: V1 cortical dynamics provide a mechanism to resolve ambiguous motion correspondence

frameworks.name: intracortical lateral propagation
frameworks.one_line: horizontal V1 connections generate traveling waves linking stimuli across space and time

core_question: How does primary visual cortex link spatiotemporally separated stimuli to represent illusory apparent motion?
key_assumptions: V1 intracortical connections can shape cortical dynamics across retinotopy
voltage-sensitive dye imaging reflects population subthreshold activity
apparent motion poses a correspondence problem the visual system must solve
manipulations: two stationary dots flashed in close spatial and temporal succession
computational model of recurrent gain control compared to imaging
key_findings: the second stimulus elicits a suppressive wave traveling toward the first's retinotopic locus
the suppressive wave emerges from a recurrent gain control fed by intracortical network
wave dynamics encode the expected motion velocity on the V1 surface
interpretations: suppressive traveling waves explain away ambiguous motion correspondence in V1
illusory motion is represented through emergent recurrent cortical dynamics
